topic.what: First published report of unidentified curved bacilli observed on gastric epithelium in patients with active chronic gastritis.
topic.how: Silver-stained histological sections from 135 gastric biopsies were examined; curved bacilli were identified in close association with the epithelial surface in patients with active chronic gastritis.
topic.why_matters: This short letter initiated the paradigm shift that gastritis and peptic ulcer disease could be infectious in origin, ultimately overturning decades of dogma attributing these conditions to stress and acid alone.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of active chronic gastritis cases with curved bacilli present
characteristics.value: Curved bacilli present in virtually all cases of active chronic gastritis examined
characteristics.vs_baseline: No prior systematic observation of bacteria on gastric mucosa in the modern era
characteristics.context: Retrospective review of gastric biopsy specimens at Royal Perth Hospital

good_for: Historical foundation of H. pylori research
Understanding the discovery process of infectious gastritis etiology

novelty: First modern identification of bacteria colonizing human gastric epithelium
Established the morphological association between curved bacilli and active chronic gastritis
Challenged the prevailing dogma that the stomach was a sterile organ due to acid pH
Opened the entire field of Helicobacter research
open_problems: Organism was unidentified and unclassified at this stage
Causation vs correlation not yet established
Culture of the organism had not yet been achieved
Mechanism of bacterial survival in gastric acid unknown
